The platform serves hundreds of merchants primarily in French-speaking Europe, requires human onboarding, and its small scale and regional focus limit its attractiveness for broad fraud campaigns. The vendor offers direct support but publishes no dedicated anti-abuse policy or automated abuse-detection system.

Weezevent.description: Weezevent is an online ticketing and event management solution that enables organizers to sell tickets, manage registrations, and track attendance for events.
Weezevent.history: Founded in April 2008 in France by Pierre-Henri Deballon and Sebastien Tonglet as a self-service online ticketing SaaS. Raised 1M EUR in 2011. Acquired Wooticket in 2012 and merged with cashless provider Nemopay in 2015, with Vente-Privee investing the same year. Regained independence and merged with Playpass in 2021. Acquired Recrewteer in 2023. Participated in Paris 2024 Olympics operations and joined French Tech Next 40 in 2024.
Weezevent.summary_abuse: Weezevent has low abuse signal typical of event ticketing platforms. Potential abuse vectors include fake-event listings and ticket-resale scams, but the platform mitigates through organizer verification, GIE Cartes Bancaires payment certification (since 2009), sequestered bank accounts for ticket funds, and CNIL-registered data processing. No major public abuse incidents are documented. Free tier exists for free events, limiting financial abuse surface. The vendor publishes a security page detailing regular audits, SSL/3DSecure encryption, and AWS hosting. Takedown responsiveness is not independently documented but the regulated payment processing environment implies standard compliance obligations.

Weglot.description: Weglot is a website translation solution facilitating automatic and manual multilingual content management for website owners and developers.
Weglot.history: Weglot was founded in Paris, France in 2016 by Augustin Prot and Remy Berda as a no-code SaaS website translation and localization platform. The company has grown to serve tens of thousands of websites and raised venture capital to scale its multilingual content management offering.
Weglot.summary_abuse: Weglot has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B translation SaaS that injects translated content into websites; it does not host user content or allow custom domain creation that would appeal to phishing operators. Its primary role is legitimate website localization.

Wehaa.description: Wehaa is a platform providing revenue management and monetization solutions designed to help media companies optimize earnings and streamline their business operations.
Wehaa.history: Founded circa 2011 as a digital revenue solutions provider for media companies in North America. Has grown over 15 years into a multi-national provider serving hundreds of media companies including the Washington Post, Los Angeles Times, and Seattle Times. No notable ownership changes or acquisitions documented.
Wehaa.summary_abuse: Wehaa is a B2B ad-tech platform serving verified media companies, which limits its abuse surface. Services are sold as white-label solutions to established media organizations, not directly to consumers. As an advertising platform, some risk of serving ads alongside questionable content exists but is mediated by the B2B media-company customer base. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Welcart.description: Welcart is a free ecommerce plugin for WordPress with top market share in Japan.
Welcart.history: Welcart was launched around 2012 as a free open-source WordPress ecommerce plugin developed by Welcart Inc. It grew to become the number-one ecommerce plugin in Japan by market share, powering over 26,000 active sites. The plugin follows a freemium model with a free core and paid extension plugins and themes. The parent company is Collne Inc. No acquisitions or major rebranding events are documented.
Welcart.summary_abuse: Welcart is an open-source WordPress ecommerce plugin with low abuse risk. As a self-hosted plugin running on the merchant's own server, it does not expose consumer-facing services that fraudsters directly abuse. Any ecommerce platform can be used for fraudulent storefronts, but Welcart's primary market is legitimate Japanese merchants. The plugin has no free hosted tier, no anonymous signup, and no mass-email features. Welcart Inc. publishes a privacy policy, privacy mark certification, and terms of service. The site also warns users about phishing emails impersonating Welcart. No major abuse incidents specifically tied to Welcart have been publicly documented.

WeltPixel Pearl Theme.description: Pearl Theme for Magento 2 by WeltPixel. Pearl Theme is following the Magento architecture, layouts and best practice in order to assure highest compatibility with 3rd party extensions.
WeltPixel Pearl Theme.history: WeltPixel was founded in 2016 by Virgil Ghic in Cluj-Napoca, Romania. Pearl Theme has remained actively maintained and was updated in January 2026.
WeltPixel Pearl Theme.summary_abuse: Pearl Theme has no notable abuse signal; it is a self-hosted Magento theme for legitimate storefronts, not hosting, shortening, or other consumer-facing infrastructure that scammers usually target.

WEN Themes Education Hub.description: WEN Themes Education Hub is a clean and elegant WordPress education theme.
WEN Themes Education Hub.history: WEN Themes Education Hub is a WordPress theme for educational institutions developed by WEN Themes, a small theme shop. No notable ownership changes or significant events on record.
WEN Themes Education Hub.summary_abuse: WEN Themes Education Hub is a WordPress theme for school websites with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted template and not a hosted service exploitable for fraud.

WEN Themes Signify Dark.description: Signify Dark is a free dark blog and corporate WordPress theme that is trendy, responsive, and dynamic by WEN Themes.
WEN Themes Signify Dark.history: Signify Dark is a free dark WordPress theme for blogs and corporate sites developed by WEN Themes. It is a free theme distributed on WordPress.org with no notable history.
WEN Themes Signify Dark.summary_abuse: WEN Themes Signify Dark is a free WordPress theme with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted template and not a hosted service.

Wepay.description: Wepay is a provider of integrated payments processing solutions tailored for platforms, facilitating transaction management across diverse service offerings.
Wepay.history: WePay was founded in 2008 as an integrated payments platform targeting software platforms and crowdfunding services. In December 2017, JPMorgan Chase completed the acquisition of WePay for a reported value of up to $400 million, making it a Chase subsidiary focused on embedded payments for small businesses and software platforms.
Wepay.summary_abuse: WePay is a payment processing platform backed by JPMorgan Chase with strong fraud controls and KYC requirements for merchant onboarding. It is occasionally implicated in fraud disputes as payment processors are, but legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates and Chase's compliance infrastructure ensures strong anti-abuse posture. No documented pattern of abuse-friendly policies.

Weply.description: Weply is a Nordic lead generation chat system designed to enhance lead generation from existing website traffic.
Weply.history: Founded in 2018 in Copenhagen. It expanded to Sweden in 2020, the Netherlands in 2022, and Norway after securing funding; no major rebrands or acquisitions are noted.
Weply.summary_abuse: Weply has limited abuse signal: as a hosted live-chat widget, it can be used on scam or impersonation landing pages to look legitimate or capture leads, but its main use is ordinary B2B lead generation. Its normal SaaS posture and published privacy policy suggest standard monitoring and support rather than abuse-friendly infrastructure.

WeTravel.description: WeTravel is a platform providing booking and payment solutions tailored for travel companies.
WeTravel.history: WeTravel was founded around 2016 as a booking and payment solution specifically for group travel organizers, tour operators, and travel companies.
WeTravel.summary_abuse: WeTravel is a travel booking and payment platform for tour operators. No notable abuse signal in public sources. Payment platforms in travel can be targets for chargeback fraud but no specific incidents are documented for this provider.

WeWeb.description: WeWeb is a no-code front-end builder that allows customers to build on-top of any back-end.
WeWeb.history: WeWeb was founded in 2019 in France as a no-code front-end builder that connects to existing backends and APIs, positioning itself as developer-friendly for building web applications without writing code.
WeWeb.summary_abuse: WeWeb is a no-code web application builder. No notable abuse signal in public sources; it is used by developers and agencies to build legitimate web applications.

Wfolio.description: Wfolio is an online solution designed to help photographers showcase their work and grow their business.
Wfolio.history: Wfolio is a website builder and cloud drive platform for photographers. The company origins appear to be Eastern European, with a customer base spread across Latvia, Ukraine, Germany, and other European countries. The platform offers portfolio websites, photo galleries, and client sales tools for photographers. The company's exact founding year and headquarters are not publicly documented.
Wfolio.summary_abuse: Wfolio is a photography portfolio website builder with a low abuse signal. The platform creates personal portfolio sites for photographers and does not offer generic free hosting under custom domains. No documented phishing, scam hosting, or fraud incidents are associated with Wfolio.

Whatfix.description: Whatfix is a SaaS based platform which provides in-app guidance and performance support for web applications and software products.
Whatfix.history: Founded in 2014 by Khadim Batti and Vara Kumar after evolving from an earlier product called SearchEnabler. It later expanded from a digital adoption platform into a multi-product suite and raised a Series E in 2024.
Whatfix.summary_abuse: Whatfix has no notable abuse signal; it is an enterprise digital adoption platform used inside customer-owned business applications rather than a public hosting, redirect, or content-publishing surface. The trust center publishes an acceptable-usage policy and vulnerability disclosure program, which points to a normal enterprise SaaS abuse posture with active enforcement rather than scam-friendly openness.

WhatsApp Business Chat.description: WhatsApp Business is a free to download app available on Android and iPhone using which businesses can connect with their customers.
WhatsApp Business Chat.history: Launched in 2018 as WhatsApp’s business-focused app and now operated under Meta as part of the WhatsApp business messaging stack.
WhatsApp Business Chat.summary_abuse: WhatsApp Business is heavily used for legitimate customer support and sales, but it is also abused for scam outreach, spam, and phishing because phone-number-based accounts can look trusted and messages reach users directly. Meta says it proactively detects and removes most spam and scam accounts, and its security.txt points to public bug-bounty and disclosure channels.

Wheel of Popups.description: Wheel of Popups is a tool that triggers an exit-intent popup designed to capture leaving visitors and support conversion optimization.
Wheel of Popups.history: Built by Earn Coupon d.o.o. as a spin-to-win Shopify popup tool, then expanded into a broader popup and lead-capture suite. The company says its founders had already started five startups, including one earlier acquisition.
Wheel of Popups.summary_abuse: Wheel of Popups is mainly a legitimate marketing popup builder, and I didn’t find a notable public abuse pattern or abuse-only usage. Like similar lead-capture tools, it could be repurposed for deceptive overlays or scam promotions on customer sites, but there’s no sign that this is a major share of use.

Wheelio.description: Wheelio is gamified pop-up/widget for ecommerce sites.
Wheelio.history: Launched in 2017 by IT Trading d.o.o.; the product has stayed under the same vendor with no notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Wheelio.summary_abuse: Wheelio has limited abuse signal overall: it is a mainstream ecommerce pop-up and lead-capture app, so scammers could misuse its custom gamified forms to dress up scam storefronts or collect emails, but the public record is dominated by normal merchant use rather than abuse. The vendor publishes a privacy policy and has a visible support presence, which lowers the risk of persistent abuse compared with anonymous page-builders or free hosting tools.

Wheely Sales.description: Wheely Sales is a tool that boosts email opt-in rates using a gamified exit intent popup designed to engage users as they leave a website.
Wheely Sales.history: Wheely Sales is a gamified exit-intent email opt-in popup tool using spin-to-win mechanics; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.
Wheely Sales.summary_abuse: Wheely Sales is a gamified email capture popup tool. Spin-to-win popups can be used as dark patterns to manipulate users into providing email addresses, but no specific fraud incidents are documented.

Whelp.description: Whelp is an AI-based omnichannel customer support platform that provides automated chatbot-driven assistance across multiple communication channels.
Whelp.history: Whelp is an omnichannel customer support SaaS platform founded by Seymur Rasulov (Whelp, Inc.). The platform provides AI-powered chatbots, shared inbox, CRM, and outbound communication tools across channels including WhatsApp, SMS, email, and social media.
Whelp.summary_abuse: Whelp is a B2B customer support platform providing omnichannel inbox, chatbot, and outbound messaging tools. As a communications platform, it enables bulk messaging across WhatsApp, Telegram, email, and Facebook Messenger. The platform requires business registration and provides standard security features. Abuse potential is moderate and consistent with other customer communication platforms.

Wherewolf.description: Wherewolf is a platform that offers capture waivers, facilitates drive bookings, and automates review processes to streamline business operations.
Wherewolf.history: Founded around 2016 in Queenstown, New Zealand by Ben Calder and Wulf Solter. Calder, running a pub crawl business, sought a mobile guest check-in solution and kept asking 'where is Wulf?' for technical help, giving the company its name. Wherewolf expanded from a local Queenstown solution to supporting thousands of tour, sports, and attraction businesses globally, processing over 200M waivers. The company opened a US office in Denver, Colorado but remains privately held with co-founders in leadership.
Wherewolf.summary_abuse: Wherewolf is a digital waiver and guest registration platform serving legitimate tour operators, attractions, and sports businesses. It has minimal abuse signal: its customer base of real, verified businesses with booking integrations makes it an unattractive vector for phishing or fraud. The platform collects PII (names, emails, IDs) and offers email/SMS marketing features, but requires business registration and typically paid plans for meaningful usage. Terms of Use clause 11 prohibits illegal or malicious use. The company has GDPR compliance measures including a published Data Processing Agreement. No notable public incidents of platform abuse by scammers have been documented.

Whistl.description: Whistl is a postal delivery company operating in the United Kingdom.
Whistl.history: Whistl traces its origins to Circular Distributors Ltd (founded 1958), a direct marketing business acquired by TNT Group in 2001. It rebranded as TNT Post in 2006 and later as Whistl in September 2014 following the spin-off of PostNL from TNT Express. After a failed expansion into final-mile delivery (suspended May 2015, with 1,800 roles affected), management completed a buyout from PostNL in October 2015. PostNL retained a 17.5% stake. The company then diversified into parcel delivery, fulfilment, and contact centre services through acquisitions including Prism DM (2017), Parcelhub (2018), Spark Ecommerce (2018), Clientbase Fulfilment (2020), and Relish (2021).
Whistl.summary_abuse: Whistl is a UK B2B logistics provider handling bulk mail, parcel delivery, and fulfilment — not a public-facing platform. Its abuse surface is limited to ordinary carrier-related fraud (lost-item scams, delivery-fee phishing) common to all postal operators. As a contracted service for established businesses with no free tier, anonymous signup, or custom-domain hosting, it offers no features that fraudsters preferentially exploit. Whistl publishes an acceptable-use policy and is regulated by UK postal authorities. No documented pattern of systematic abuse specific to this provider was identified.

Whitelabel MD.description: White Label MD is a platform for creating ecommerce telemedicine websites with on-demand prescription fulfillment capabilities.
Whitelabel MD.history: Founded 2022 as Whitelabel MD LLC. Provides a white-label turnkey platform for creating e-commerce telemedicine websites with on-demand prescription fulfillment. Integrates telemedicine providers with nationwide mail-order pharmacies. Team has over 30 years combined experience in healthcare services and technology. LegitScript verified for healthcare compliance.
Whitelabel MD.summary_abuse: Whitelabel MD is a white-label telemedicine e-commerce platform enabling creation of online prescription fulfillment websites. The platform handles prescription medications, which carries inherent regulatory and abuse risks including potential for unlicensed prescribing or controlled substance diversion. The company uses LegitScript verification and has privacy and terms policies in place. As a turnkey provider powering other businesses' telemedicine sites, abuse depends on how downstream operators use the platform. No notable public abuse incidents have been documented.

Whitespark SEO.description: Aims to help improve rankings and drive business via Google Search.
Whitespark SEO.history: Founded in 2005 by Darren Shaw in Edmonton, Alberta, Canada, initially offering web design and SEO services. Over two decades it grew into a provider of local SEO software and managed services, serving over 100,000 enterprises, agencies, and small businesses globally. No acquisitions, rebrands, or deprecation events have occurred.
Whitespark SEO.summary_abuse: Whitespark's SEO services have minimal abuse signal. The company explicitly excludes high-risk industries (garage door repair, locksmiths, adult services) from its client roster and publishes a terms of service and privacy policy. Its core offerings — Google Business Profile management, citation building, and rank tracking — are legitimate local SEO services with no documented pattern of fraud or scam facilitation. No notable abuse incidents or security-research reports identified.

WHMCS.description: WHMCS is an automation platform that simplifies and automates all aspects of operating an online web hosting and domain registrar business.
WHMCS.history: WHMCS was founded in 2005 by Matt Pugh in the UK as a billing and automation platform for web hosting companies. It became the dominant hosting billing solution and was acquired by Web.com (now Newfold Digital) in 2012.
WHMCS.summary_abuse: WHMCS is web hosting billing and automation software widely used by hosting resellers and small hosting companies. It has been targeted by hackers seeking to steal customer payment data, and some illegitimate hosting resellers use it to operate infrastructure for spam or fraud. WHMCS maintains a security disclosure program and releases patches promptly, but its widespread deployment in the hosting industry means it sees occasional exploitation.

Wholesale Suite.description: Wholesale Suite is a plugin that enables businesses to manage and streamline wholesale operations online, supporting pricing, ordering, and workflow efficiency.
Wholesale Suite.history: Released in 2015 by Josh Kohlbach as a free WooCommerce wholesale pricing plugin on WordPress.org. Expanded into a commercial suite of five premium plugins (Wholesale Prices Premium, Order Form, Lead Capture, Payments, Quotes) under Rymera Web Co Pty Ltd, an Australian company. No ownership changes, acquisitions, forks, or deprecation events since launch.
Wholesale Suite.summary_abuse: Wholesale Suite is a B2B WooCommerce plugin for managing wholesale pricing and orders on self-hosted WordPress stores. It has no notable abuse signal: it is a backend plugin with no consumer-facing hosting surface, no free-tier custom-domain support, no anonymous signup, and no mass-email or URL-redirect capabilities. Security vulnerabilities are managed through a Patchstack responsible-disclosure program. No documented history of significant abuse patterns or fraud campaigns leveraging this specific plugin.

Whooshkaa.history: Whooshkaa was an Australian podcast hosting and analytics platform. It was acquired by Megaphone, which is owned by Spotify. The whooshkaa.com domain now redirects to Megaphone's enterprise podcast platform, and the original Whooshkaa product has been deprecated in favor of Megaphone by Spotify.
Whooshkaa.summary_abuse: Whooshkaa was an Australian podcast hosting and analytics platform, later acquired by Megaphone (Spotify). As a B2B podcast platform, it served legitimate content creators. The original product is now deprecated and folded into Spotify's Megaphone platform. No notable abuse incidents documented.

WhosOn.description: WhosOn is a secure live chat and AI platform for managing and automating customer communications.
WhosOn.history: Founded in 2002 by Parker Software (UK) as a live chat platform for enterprise businesses. Developed and supported in-house since inception. Added AI-powered chatbot capabilities and ChatGPT integration in recent years. No acquisitions, ownership changes, or deprecation events.
WhosOn.summary_abuse: WhosOn has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B live chat SaaS targeting regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government) with enterprise clients. The platform offers on-premises deployment, has no consumer-facing free tier with custom domains, and requires sales engagement for setup. No documented public incidents of abuse or fraud associated with the platform.

Whova.description: Whova is an all-in-one event management platform supporting in-person, hybrid, and virtual events.
Whova.history: Whova was founded around 2012, originally as a people-search and social-connection app. Around 2014-2015 the company pivoted to event management software, launching a mobile event app and platform for in-person, hybrid, and virtual events. The company was founded by Dr. Yuanyuan Zhou, a computer science professor at UC San Diego, and is headquartered in San Diego, California. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events have occurred since.
Whova.summary_abuse: Whova is an event management SaaS platform with very limited abuse surface. The platform requires a paid organizer account with verified billing and contact information, making anonymous signup impossible. It does not offer a free tier, custom-domain hosting for arbitrary content, or any consumer-facing file-hosting or URL-redirect features that fraudsters commonly exploit. No documented pattern of phishing hosting, malware distribution, or scam activity through Whova exists in public security research. Typical abuse vectors for event platforms (fake event pages, phishing ticket sales) are not reported for Whova. The vendor maintains standard terms of use and privacy policies.

Wicked Reports.description: Wicked Reports is a marketing analytics platform that provides subscription-based, actionable reports on marketing results at each stage of the customer's journey from first click to sale.
Wicked Reports.history: Wicked Reports was founded in 2014 by Scott Desgrosseilliers after his friend Mark Murrell struggled to measure Facebook ad ROI for his e-commerce lobster business. Desgrosseilliers, a former database administrator at Apartments.com, built the first prototype as a pivot-table-based attribution system. The company launched as a SaaS platform in 2015 and grew to $1.2M ARR by 2017. It remains independently owned as Wicked Reports LLC, headquartered in Salem, Massachusetts.
Wicked Reports.summary_abuse: Wicked Reports has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B marketing-attribution SaaS for e-commerce brands, requiring a paid subscription ($499/mo minimum) and having no free tier, no anonymous signup, no user-generated content hosting, and no URL-shortening or mass-email features. The platform connects via server-side APIs to CRM and ad platforms, not as a consumer-facing service that fraudsters could weaponize. No documented patterns of phishing, scam hosting, or malware distribution associated with the platform.

Widde.description: Widde is a platform that enables the use of interactive ecommerce videos on webpages, enhancing the shopping experience and providing businesses with a competitive advantage.
Widde.history: Founded in 2021 by Raísa Bresolin and Pedro Ferreira in Brazil to bring Video Commerce to the Brazilian e-commerce market during the Covid-19 pandemic. The company is the first Video Commerce solution in Brazil. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, or deprecation events since founding.
Widde.summary_abuse: Widde is a Video Commerce SaaS platform that embeds interactive video players on e-commerce websites. It has no notable abuse signal — it is a targeted B2B tool for Brazilian e-commerce merchants with no consumer-facing hosting, URL-redirect, mass-email, or file-hosting features that fraudsters typically exploit. The service requires merchant account integration rather than anonymous signup and does not support arbitrary content hosting under custom domains. No documented abuse incidents or security research reports were found.

WideBundle.description: WideBundle is a Shopify application that allows a merchant to set up bundles on his store.
WideBundle.history: Founded in 2020 by Mat De Sousa as a Shopify product-bundling app. Ogi joined as the first employee the same year. The team grew to 7 by 2025, serving over 2,500 merchants across 37 countries. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
WideBundle.summary_abuse: WideBundle is a Shopify app for product bundling with no notable abuse signal. It embeds on store product pages to display bundle offers and has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters target — it does not host user-generated content, offer URL redirection, or enable anonymous signup. As a commercial Shopify app serving verified merchants, abuse risk is negligible.

Widen.description: Widen is a digital asset management and product information management solutions provider.
Widen.history: Widen was founded in 1948 in Madison, Wisconsin as Widen Engraving Co., a plate-engraving business for newspapers. It transformed over decades into a digital asset management company, rebranding as Widen Enterprises in 1990. CEO Matthew Gonnering (appointed 2009) steered the company toward a SaaS model. In September 2021, Widen was acquired by Acquia (majority-owned by Vista Equity Partners) and rebranded as Acquia DAM.
Widen.summary_abuse: Acquia DAM (Widen) has no notable abuse signal. It is a closed SaaS digital asset management platform used by enterprises to organize brand assets. The product has no consumer-facing surface, no free-tier custom-domain hosting, and no anonymous signup. Acquia maintains a published responsible disclosure policy and HIPAA-ready posture. Abuse risk is limited to the remote possibility of customers uploading infringing content to shared portals, which Acquia addresses through standard AUP enforcement and takedown procedures.

WiderPlanet.description: WiderPlanet is a real-time advertising platform that utilises user behaviour data to deliver targeted advertisements.
WiderPlanet.history: Founded in 2010 in Seoul, South Korea as Artist Company (CEO: Gyeong-ju Hwang). Launched the TargetingGates DSP/DMP platform in 2012 under the WiderPlanet brand. Listed on KOSDAQ in 2021 and acquired e-commerce company IBL the same year.
WiderPlanet.summary_abuse: WiderPlanet is a Korean B2B ad-tech platform (DSP/DMP/RTB) with no notable abuse signal in security research. Its TargetingGates platform collects behavioral data via cookies and anonymous identifiers for targeted advertising, but lacks the consumer-facing surfaces typically exploited for phishing or scams. The company offers opt-out mechanisms and states it does not target minors under 14 or link data to sensitive categories. As an invite-only B2B platform with sales-gated access, automated abuse vectors are minimal.

WidgetWhats.description: WidgetWhats is a fully customizable chat widget with appearance, text, color, button style and position.
WidgetWhats.history: WidgetWhats was founded around 2020 as a hosted SaaS WhatsApp chat widget for websites. It offers a free tier and paid plans differentiated only by the number of widgets. No notable ownership changes, acquisitions, forks, or deprecation events since launch.
WidgetWhats.summary_abuse: WidgetWhats is a niche SaaS chat widget that opens WhatsApp click-to-chat conversations from a website. It does not host user-generated content, provide URL redirection, or allow anonymous signup. The vendor publishes a content policy prohibiting illegal and abusive use. No notable public abuse incidents or security research reports are documented. The widget's abuse surface is minimal, as it simply connects visitors to a business's WhatsApp number.

Wigzo.description: Wigzo is e-commerce marketing automation platform that helps businesses of every size dig deeper into data to find opportunities to increase their sales and revenue.
Wigzo.history: Wigzo was founded in 2014 by Umair Mohammed, Shamail Tayyab, and Mohammad Atyab in Haryana, India as an AI-driven marketing automation platform for e-commerce businesses. In January 2022, Shiprocket, a Zomato-backed logistics aggregator, acquired a 75% stake in Wigzo at a valuation of approximately $25 million.
Wigzo.summary_abuse: Wigzo has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B marketing automation platform targeting e-commerce businesses in India with email, SMS, and WhatsApp campaign tools. These mass-communication features carry inherent spam risk if misused, but Wigzo is not documented as a preferred tool for spammers and operates with standard anti-spam policies.

Wijmo.description: Wijmo is a JavaScript UI component library built in TypeScript, ideal for developing lightweight, high-speed HTML5/JavaScript applications with no dependencies, and designed for enterprise use.
Wijmo.history: Wijmo was first released in 2010 by ComponentOne as a jQuery UI widget library for web developers. It was rebuilt from scratch in TypeScript in 2014 to support modern frameworks including Angular, React, and Vue. ComponentOne was acquired by GrapeCity in 2012; the product is now sold under the MESCIUS brand, the developer tools division of GrapeCity (Japan). No notable ownership changes, forks, deprecation events, or licensing controversies since.
Wijmo.summary_abuse: Wijmo is a commercial JavaScript UI component library with no notable abuse signal. It is a client-side development library with no consumer-facing surface, no content-hosting features, no free tier, and no anonymous signup capabilities. There are no documented abuse patterns unique to Wijmo; fraudsters have no reason to specifically target this technology. It is occasionally used as a component in legitimate enterprise web applications, which is the extent of its abuse surface.

Wiki.js.description: Wiki.js is a wiki engine running on Node.js and written in JavaScript.
Wiki.js.history: Wiki.js was created by Nicolas Giard (NGPixel) under the Requarks.io organization and first released in 2017. It gained popularity as a modern, extensible open-source wiki platform built on Node.js with support for multiple database backends, enterprise authentication, and Git-based content sync. The project has accumulated over 28,000 GitHub stars and 100 million downloads with sponsorship from Cloudflare, DigitalOcean, GitHub, and others.
Wiki.js.summary_abuse: Wiki.js is a self-hosted open-source wiki engine deployed on the user's own infrastructure. As a documentation platform with no consumer-facing hosted service, it has no meaningful abuse surface. The software is AGPL-3.0 licensed with no telemetry, tracking, or data collection by default.

Wikinggruppen.description: Wikinggruppen is a Swedish company specializing in ecommerce solutions, offering custom platform development, CMS integration, and technical support.
Wikinggruppen.history: Founded in 1998 by Jonte Bergman. Launched its WGR ecommerce platform in 2005. Acquired by Visma in 2018. In April 2026, the management team (Albin Jansson, Caroline Berg, Johan Söderberg) together with founder Jonte Bergman and Monitor ERP bought the company back from Visma, returning it to private, independent ownership with local control in Hudiksvall, Sweden.
Wikinggruppen.summary_abuse: Wikinggruppen has no notable abuse signal. It is a Swedish B2B/B2C ecommerce SaaS platform serving a known mid-market merchant base primarily in Sweden. The platform offers a 30-day free trial and supports custom domains, but its small market and known customer base limit appeal for large-scale fraudulent use. No public incidents of abuse are documented. The vendor operates with responsive support channels and standard terms of service.

Wikit Live Chat.description: Wikit Live Chat is a platform that enables the creation of intelligent chatbots, leveraging the capabilities of generative AI to enhance user interactions.
Wikit Live Chat.history: Wikit was founded in 2017 in Lyon, France, as a no-code generative AI chatbot platform. The company positions itself as a sovereign AI provider integrating various LLMs including Azure OpenAI, GPT, and Mistral.
Wikit Live Chat.summary_abuse: Wikit Live Chat is an enterprise AI chatbot SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B platform targeting enterprises for internal knowledge management and customer support automation, with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

WikkaWiki.description: WikkaWiki is an open-source wiki application written in PHP.
WikkaWiki.history: WikkaWiki was created by Jason Tourtelotte in May 2004 as a fork of WakkaWiki after development on the original project was discontinued in 2003. It is a lightweight PHP wiki engine using MySQL for storage, released under the GPL license. Development has been largely dormant for many years with no meaningful updates.
WikkaWiki.summary_abuse: WikkaWiki has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted wiki engine with no SaaS component, and its niche user base and dormant development status mean it carries no meaningful abuse footprint.

WildJar.description: WildJar is a call tracking and intelligence platform which helps you understand where your leads are coming from, who is calling you, what your conversations are about and connect that data into other platforms.
WildJar.history: WildJar was founded in 2016 in Sydney, Australia, as a call tracking and analytics platform. The company primarily serves the Australian and UK markets, reaching $5.4M in revenue by 2024.
WildJar.summary_abuse: WildJar is a call tracking platform with no notable abuse signal. Its services are oriented toward legitimate marketing analytics and it has no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would typically target.

Windows CE.description: Windows CE is an operating system designed for small footprint devices or embedded systems.
Windows CE.history: Windows CE was launched by Microsoft in November 1996 as a compact embedded operating system for handheld and mobile devices. It was renamed Windows Embedded CE in 2006 and Windows Embedded Compact in 2011. Microsoft officially discontinued support, and it is now a deprecated platform replaced by modern embedded Windows variants.
Windows CE.summary_abuse: Windows CE has no notable abuse signal as a commercial embedded OS product. It is a deprecated proprietary operating system used in industrial and legacy devices with no consumer-facing surface attractive to fraudsters.

Windows Server.description: Windows Server is a brand name for a group of server operating systems.
Windows Server.history: Windows Server originated as Windows NT 3.1 Advanced Server released in 1993. Microsoft rebranded the line as Windows Server with the 2003 release. It has evolved through Server 2008, 2012, 2016, 2019, 2022, and 2025, each aligned with corresponding Windows desktop releases. It remains the dominant commercial server OS in enterprise environments.
Windows Server.summary_abuse: Windows Server itself has no meaningful abuse signal as a product — it is a legitimate enterprise OS. However, compromised or misconfigured Windows Server instances are commonly targeted in ransomware, credential-stuffing, and lateral movement attacks. Microsoft maintains an active vulnerability disclosure and patching program (MSRC) with a public bug bounty.

WINEAROUND.description: WINEAROUND is a booking software that enables businesses to manage online and offline reservations, schedule appointments, streamline operations, and enhance customer service for guided tours and activities.
WINEAROUND.history: WINEAROUND is a booking and reservation management SaaS focused on wine tours and guided activities, originating in Italy. It targets small hospitality businesses offering wine-related experiences.
WINEAROUND.summary_abuse: WINEAROUND is a niche booking software for wine tourism businesses with no documented abuse signal. Its B2B focus and vertical-specific nature limit exposure to common fraud patterns.

WineDirect.description: WineDirect is an all-in-one ecommerce and POS (Point of Sale) platform that is specifically designed for wineries and wine retailers.
WineDirect.history: WineDirect is a winery-focused ecommerce and management platform serving over 1,600 wineries. Its DTC SaaS division was acquired by Fullsteam, which now drives the platform's continued development. WineDirect Classic remains available for existing clients through end of 2026.
WineDirect.summary_abuse: WineDirect has no notable abuse signal. It is a specialized B2B SaaS platform for wineries focused on direct-to-consumer sales and compliance, with no consumer-facing features that would attract fraud or phishing activity.

Wink.description: Wink Toolkit is a JavaScript toolkit used to build mobile web apps.
Wink.history: Wink Toolkit is a JavaScript framework for building mobile web applications. It was an early mobile web framework that has since become largely obsolete as the mobile web matured with more capable tools like React and Angular.
Wink.summary_abuse: Wink Toolkit has no abuse signal. It is a dormant JavaScript mobile framework library with no consumer-facing surface.

WinLocal.description: WinLocal is a monitoring and feedback collection service that helps businesses track reviews and improve visibility on maps and search results.
WinLocal.history: WinLocal is a digital marketing platform for local businesses, offering local search optimization and digital business cards. Founded around 2019, it is US-based though some versions appear to operate in German-speaking markets.
WinLocal.summary_abuse: WinLocal has no notable abuse signal. It is a local business marketing tool with no consumer-facing features that would make it a target for fraud or scam infrastructure.

Wins eCommerce.description: Wins eCommerce is a scalable ecommerce infrastructure that enables online store creation and product sales.

Wins eCommerce.summary_abuse: Wins eCommerce is an e-commerce platform. Storefront platforms can be used to create fake shops, but no specific documented abuse incidents exist for Wins eCommerce.

Winstone Servlet Container.history: Winstone is a lightweight Java servlet container originally developed to provide servlet functionality without J2EE bloat. It is bundled in the Jenkins WAR file and has since been maintained by the Jenkins project, with later versions delegating HTTP handling to Jetty internally.
Winstone Servlet Container.summary_abuse: Winstone Servlet Container has no abuse signal. It is a backend Java servlet engine used primarily as an embedded server within Jenkins, with no consumer-facing surface.

Winter CMS.description: Winter CMS is an open source content management framework built on the Laravel PHP framework for rapid development.
Winter CMS.history: Winter CMS was forked from October CMS in March 2021 following a philosophical disagreement between core maintainers and October's founders over open-source governance. It is built on the Laravel PHP framework and led by Luke Towers along with community contributors, maintaining the MIT license.
Winter CMS.summary_abuse: Winter CMS has no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted open-source CMS, its security posture depends on site operators, but the project itself carries no documented abuse footprint.

Wipe Analytics.description: Wipe Analytics provides user behavior analysis for high-traffic and dynamically generated websites.
Wipe Analytics.history: Wipe Analytics is a German analytics platform specialising in user behavior analysis for high-traffic and dynamically generated websites.
Wipe Analytics.summary_abuse: Wipe Analytics is a niche German web analytics tool with no notable abuse signal.

Wiqhit.description: Wiqhit is an online personalization tool that customizes website content to engage visitors and increase conversions through tailored user experiences.
Wiqhit.history: Wiqhit is a website personalization tool for visitor engagement and conversion optimization; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.

Wirecard.description: Wirecard is a defunct German payment processor and financial services provider.
Wirecard.history: Wirecard was founded in 1999 in Munich, Germany, as a payment processor and financial services provider. It grew rapidly to become a DAX-listed company but collapsed in June 2020 after revealing that €1.9 billion in supposed cash reserves did not exist. CEO Markus Braun was arrested; COO Jan Marsalek fled to Belarus. The company filed for insolvency, and criminal trials against executives have continued through 2026. It is one of the largest corporate fraud scandals in German history.
Wirecard.summary_abuse: Wirecard is the center of one of Europe's largest corporate fraud cases. The company engaged in large-scale accounting fraud and money laundering, fabricating €1.9 billion in cash reserves. Its payment infrastructure was also linked to processing transactions for illicit merchants. The company is now insolvent and its services deprecated, but historical use of Wirecard as a payment gateway by scam operations was documented by investigators.

Wiremo.description: Wiremo is a customer review platform designed for eCommerce businesses to collect, manage, and display user-generated feedback.
Wiremo.history: Wiremo is a customer review and testimonial platform for e-commerce businesses, offering review collection, management, and display widgets. It is a small independent SaaS with no notable ownership events documented.

WiserNotify.summary_abuse: WiserNotify has low abuse signal. Social proof notifications can be used to create false urgency (e.g., fake purchase counters), which constitutes a mild dark pattern, but the platform is not documented as a tool for fraud or phishing campaigns.

Wistia.description: Wistia is designed exclusively to serve companies using video on their websites for marketing, support, and sales.
Wistia.history: Wistia was founded in 2006 in Cambridge, Massachusetts as a video hosting and marketing platform built specifically for business use. The company has remained bootstrapped and independent, growing steadily without external venture funding to serve marketing and sales teams at thousands of companies.
Wistia.summary_abuse: Wistia has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B video hosting platform with mandatory account registration, no anonymous uploads, and content intended for legitimate marketing and sales use. The platform holds SOC 2 Type 2 certification and maintains an active security posture.

wisyCMS.history: wisyCMS is a German website content management system developed by 3We, a web development company based in Germany. Limited public information is available about its founding date.

Witbooking.description: Witbooking is a hotel booking engine that enables the management of reservations, availability, and pricing through an online platform.
Witbooking.history: Witbooking is a hotel booking engine from Spain that allows hotels to manage direct reservations online. It serves the hospitality industry with availability management and pricing tools.
Witbooking.summary_abuse: Witbooking has no notable abuse signal. It is a niche hotel booking engine SaaS serving the hospitality industry with no features that attract fraud or phishing.

With Reach.description: With Reach is a fintech/payments service provider that helps retailers connect with customers around the world.
With Reach.history: With Reach is a fintech payments service provider helping online retailers accept payments globally with localized methods. It serves cross-border ecommerce merchants.
With Reach.summary_abuse: With Reach has low abuse signal as a B2B cross-border payments platform. Payment processors carry inherent risk of misuse by fraudulent merchants, but With Reach operates in the B2B space with merchant vetting.

Wix.description: Wix provides cloud-based web development services, allowing users to create HTML5 websites and mobile sites.
Wix.history: Wix was founded in 2006 in Tel Aviv, Israel, by Avishai Abrahami, Nadav Abrahami, and Giora Kaplan, aiming to simplify website creation. The platform moved from Flash to HTML5 in 2012 and went public on Nasdaq in November 2013, raising $127M. Wix grew to over 200 million registered users and became one of the dominant website builders globally.
Wix.summary_abuse: Wix is a major website builder platform with a documented abuse pattern: its free tier with custom subdomain support (site.wixsite.com) is frequently exploited by phishing campaigns, fake storefronts, and scam landing pages. Researchers have documented Wix-hosted phishing pages targeting banking and service brands. The free tier allows account creation with minimal KYC and rapid site creation, lowering the barrier for fraudsters. Wix maintains an AUP and abuse reporting channel but takedown speed has drawn criticism. Abuse is a small but meaningful fraction of total platform usage.

Wix Answers.description: Wix Answers is a cloud-based help desk software.
Wix Answers.history: Wix Answers is a cloud-based help desk and customer support platform developed by Wix and launched around 2018. It is part of the broader Wix ecosystem and targets SMBs and mid-market companies needing self-service knowledge bases and ticketing.
Wix Answers.summary_abuse: Wix Answers has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B help desk SaaS product with no consumer-facing surface that attracts fraud.

Wix eCommerce.history: Wix eCommerce is the online store functionality built into the Wix platform, which was founded in 2006 in Tel Aviv, Israel. Wix has continuously expanded its ecommerce capabilities as part of its overall website builder platform. Wix.com is publicly traded on Nasdaq.
Wix eCommerce.summary_abuse: Wix eCommerce shares the same abuse profile as the core Wix platform. Free and low-cost Wix stores have been used to set up fake storefronts and scam pages. The ease of store creation with minimal verification makes it attractive to fraudsters building counterfeit product listings or phishing-adjacent checkout pages.

Wized.description: Wized is a web application builder that enables the creation of apps without requiring any coding knowledge.
Wized.history: Wized is a no-code web app builder that allows creating applications without coding, layering data and logic on top of Webflow designs. It is a small startup product launched around 2020.
Wized.summary_abuse: Wized has no notable abuse signal. It is a no-code app builder for developers and designers with no consumer-facing attack surface for fraudsters.

WiziShop.description: WiziShop is an ecommerce solution provider.

WiziShop.summary_abuse: WiziShop has low abuse signal. As an ecommerce platform, it carries baseline risk of being used to set up fake storefronts, but its B2B orientation and paid-only tiers reduce the likelihood of large-scale fraud exploitation.

Wizpay.description: Wizpay is a buy now pay later solution.
Wizpay.history: Wizpay is an Australian buy now, pay later (BNPL) service that allows consumers to split purchases into four fortnightly installments. It does not conduct credit checks at signup. Wizpay focuses on Australian retailers.
Wizpay.summary_abuse: Wizpay carries moderate abuse risk inherent to BNPL services. The no-credit-check signup model and instant account creation lower barriers to fraudulent use, including use of stolen identity information to make purchases. BNPL platforms generally face chargeback fraud and identity fraud risks. No specific large-scale incidents are documented for Wizpay.

WizSoft.description: WizSoft is a search engine designed for embedding in ecommerce websites and other platforms where data are stored in records.
WizSoft.history: WizSoft is an embeddable search engine for e-commerce websites and data-rich platforms; no notable founding date or ownership events are documented.

Wodify.summary_abuse: Wodify has no notable abuse signal. It is a gym management SaaS used by fitness studios with no consumer-facing attack surface for fraudsters.

Wolfeo.summary_abuse: Wolfeo has no notable abuse signal. It is a digital product sales platform primarily serving coaches and educators with no features that attract large-scale fraud.

WolframAlpha.description: WolframAlpha is a tool that integrates live computational knowledge into blogs or websites.
WolframAlpha.history: WolframAlpha was launched on May 18, 2009, by Wolfram Research, founded by mathematician and programmer Stephen Wolfram. Wolters Kluwer.description: Wolters Kluwer is a site builder designed for tax professionals to create and manage websites tailored to their practice needs.

Wolters Kluwer.summary_abuse: The Wolters Kluwer website builder for tax professionals has no notable abuse signal. It is a specialized tool for licensed professionals with no consumer-facing features that attract fraud.

Woltlab Community Framework.description: WoltLab Community Framework is a modular and extensible software platform designed for building and managing online communities with features like forums, blogs, and galleries.
Woltlab Community Framework.history: WoltLab Community Framework (WCF) is a German-developed open-source PHP framework for building community and forum software. It powers WoltLab Suite (formerly Burning Board), one of the older German forum platforms dating to the early 2000s.
Woltlab Community Framework.summary_abuse: WoltLab Community Framework has low abuse signal. As a self-hosted forum software framework, it can be used to create communities that host abusive content, but the project itself has no documented systematic abuse footprint.

Wonder.description: Wonder is an analytics and marketing tool that uses offline and online data to implement optimal marketing strategies aimed at achieving website goals, such as conversions.
Wonder.history: Wonder (wonder-ma.com) is a Japanese analytics and marketing automation platform combining offline and online data to optimise website conversions. No public ownership changes are documented.

WooChat.description: WooChat is a marketing automation tool that helps store owners to grow their business.

WooCommerce.description: WooCommerce is an open-source ecommerce plugin for WordPress.
WooCommerce.history: WooCommerce was created by WooThemes founders Mark Forrester, Magnus Jepson, and Adii Pienaar and launched on September 27, 2011 as a free WordPress ecommerce plugin. In May 2015, Automattic acquired WooThemes and the plugin, renaming it WooCommerce. It grew to power over 30% of all ecommerce sites globally and remains the most widely used ecommerce platform worldwide.
WooCommerce.summary_abuse: WooCommerce itself is a legitimate open-source ecommerce plugin, but the massive number of WooCommerce-powered stores means it is frequently seen on fake storefront and scam sites. Attackers set up counterfeit product stores on WooCommerce to collect payment credentials. The plugin's open-source nature means security depends heavily on site operator hygiene.
