Woopra.history: Woopra was founded in 2008 as a customer journey analytics platform providing real-time analytics on customer behavior across web and mobile. It has maintained independent operation.
Woopra.summary_abuse: Woopra has no notable abuse signal. It is a customer analytics SaaS with no consumer-facing features attractive to fraudsters.

Woorise.description: Woorise is a marketing platform that enables the creation of lead generation landing pages.
Woorise.history: Woorise was founded in 2016 by Christopher Vasiliou in Athens, Greece. It is a bootstrapped marketing platform enabling creation of landing pages, forms, surveys, quizzes, and viral giveaways for lead generation.
Woorise.summary_abuse: Woorise is a lead generation and giveaway platform with a free tier. Giveaway platforms carry minor abuse risk through fake contest entries, but no specific documented misuse of Woorise is known.

Woostify.description: Woostify is fast, lightweight, responsive and flexible WooCommerce theme built with SEO, speed, and usability in mind.
Woostify.history: Woostify is a fast, lightweight WooCommerce theme primarily targeting e-commerce store owners. It is built in Vietnam and distributed through WordPress theme repositories.
Woostify.summary_abuse: Woostify is a WooCommerce theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted theme with no hosted infrastructure for fraudsters to exploit.

Wootric.description: Wootric is a customer feedback platform designed to enhance customer experience management for digital applications and B2B SaaS.
Wootric.history: Wootric was founded in 2013 by Deepa Subramanian and Jessica Pfeifer to provide in-app customer feedback and NPS measurement for digital products. InMoment acquired Wootric in January 2021 to bolster its digital feedback and CX improvement platform.
Wootric.summary_abuse: Wootric is a B2B customer feedback and NPS platform with no notable abuse signal. Following its 2021 acquisition by InMoment, it is used primarily by enterprise customers.

WoowUp.description: WoowUp is a tool of CRM and predictive marketing.
WoowUp.history: WoowUp is an Argentine CRM and predictive marketing platform targeting retail and e-commerce businesses in Latin America. No notable ownership changes are documented.
WoowUp.summary_abuse: WoowUp is a niche CRM and marketing tool for Latin American retailers with no notable abuse signal.

Word of Mouth.description: Word of Mouth is a feedback platform that allows users to compare reviews and ratings for various services and businesses.
Word of Mouth.history: Word of Mouth is an Australian review and feedback platform allowing users to compare ratings across various services and businesses.
Word of Mouth.summary_abuse: Word of Mouth has no notable abuse signal. It is a review aggregation platform that may occasionally be targeted by fake review campaigns, but this is a general industry issue rather than a specific platform vulnerability.

WordAds.description: WordAds is an advertising platform run by Automatic that allows bloggers and website owners to place advertisements on their blogs and websites.
WordAds.history: WordAds is the advertising platform launched by Automattic around 2012 for WordPress.com and self-hosted WordPress sites. It competes with Google AdSense for WordPress publisher monetization.
WordAds.summary_abuse: WordAds is an advertising network with the typical ad-platform risk of serving malicious or misleading ads (malvertising). Automattic maintains controls over ad content but the platform shares baseline ad-network abuse risks.

Wordfence.description: Wordfence is a security plugin for sites that use WordPress. Wordfence includes an endpoint firewall and malware scanner.
Wordfence.history: Wordfence was founded in 2012 by Mark Maunder and is developed by Defiant Inc., headquartered in Seattle, Washington. It is the most widely used WordPress security plugin with over 5 million active installations. It offers a free tier and a paid premium version with real-time threat intelligence.
Wordfence.summary_abuse: Wordfence is a security tool rather than an abuse vector. It has no notable misuse pattern; its purpose is to protect WordPress sites against malware, brute-force attacks, and intrusions.

Wordfence Login Security.description: Wordfence Login Security contains a subset of the functionality found in the full Wordfence plugin: Two-factor Authentication, XML-RPC Protection and Login Page CAPTCHA.
Wordfence Login Security.history: Wordfence was founded in 2012 by Mark Maunder and is developed by Defiant Inc., headquartered in Seattle, Washington. It is the most widely used WordPress security plugin with over 5 million active installations. It offers a free tier and a paid premium version with real-time threat intelligence.
Wordfence Login Security.summary_abuse: Wordfence is a security tool rather than an abuse vector. It has no notable misuse pattern; its purpose is to protect WordPress sites against malware, brute-force attacks, and intrusions.

WordLift.description: WordLift is an AI tool that analyzes content, identifies key business topics, and enables the insertion of semantic markup without needing technical skills.
WordLift.history: WordLift is an Italian AI-powered SEO plugin for WordPress launched around 2014. It uses knowledge graphs and structured data to enhance content SEO.
WordLift.summary_abuse: WordLift has no notable abuse signal. It is an AI SEO tool for WordPress with no consumer-facing attack surface for fraudsters.

WordPress.description: WordPress is a free and open-source content management system written in PHP and paired with a MySQL or MariaDB database. Features include a plugin architecture and a template system.
WordPress.history: WordPress was created by Matt Mullenweg and Mike Little in 2003 as a fork of b2/cafelog. It has grown to power over 43% of all websites globally. The WordPress Foundation holds the trademark while Automattic, led by Mullenweg, is the primary commercial backer. WordPress 5.0 (2018) introduced the Gutenberg block editor, a major architectural shift. In 2024, a high-profile dispute between Mullenweg and WP Engine sparked community controversy.
WordPress.summary_abuse: WordPress powers 43% of the web, making it the most-targeted CMS platform. Attackers routinely exploit outdated WordPress installations for malware distribution, phishing page hosting, and SEO spam injection. The platform itself is not abusive, but its massive scale, open-source plugin ecosystem with inconsistent security, and large number of unpatched sites make WordPress a frequent vector. The WordPress Security Team maintains a responsible disclosure process.

WordPress Block Editor.description: Sites using the WordPress Block Editor, also known as Gutenberg.
WordPress Block Editor.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Block Editor.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Default.description: WordPress Default is a default WordPress theme.
WordPress Default.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Default.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Multisite.description: A multisite network is a collection of sites that all share the same WordPress installation core files.
WordPress Multisite.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Multisite.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Popular Posts.description: WordPress Popular Posts is a plugin for the WordPress content management system that allows users to display a widget or shortcode featuring a list of the most popular posts on their website based on criteria such as pageviews or comments.
WordPress Popular Posts.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Popular Posts.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Project Manager.description: WordPress Project Manager is an open-source project management tool designed to organize tasks and ensure timely project delivery.
WordPress Project Manager.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Project Manager.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Site Editor.description: Full Site Editing enables users to design and customize their entire WordPress website with a block-based editor.
WordPress Site Editor.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Site Editor.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress Super Cache.description: WordPress Super Cache is a static caching plugin for WordPress.
WordPress Super Cache.history: Part of the WordPress open-source project ecosystem, developed and maintained by Automattic and the WordPress community since 2003.
WordPress Super Cache.summary_abuse: As part of the WordPress ecosystem, this component shares the platform abuse risk where WordPress installations are commonly targeted by attackers. The component itself has no specific abuse pattern.

WordPress VIP.description: WordPress VIP is a managed hosting platform for WordPress.
WordPress VIP.history: WordPress VIP is the enterprise managed hosting and platform service from Automattic for high-traffic publishers and enterprises. It launched around 2007 and serves major media organizations and Fortune 500 companies requiring high-availability WordPress deployments with SLAs.
WordPress VIP.summary_abuse: WordPress VIP has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise-grade managed hosting platform with strict vetting and enterprise contracts that make it unattractive for fraudsters.

WordPress.com.description: WordPress.com is a platform for self-publishing that is popular for blogging and other works.
WordPress.com.history: WordPress.com is the hosted version of WordPress launched by Automattic in 2005. It offers free and paid hosting for WordPress sites. Automattic was founded by WordPress co-creator Matt Mullenweg in 2005. WordPress.com is separate from the open-source WordPress.org software.
WordPress.com.summary_abuse: WordPress.com shares abuse characteristics with other major site-builder platforms. Its free tier has been used for phishing pages, spam blogs, and fake storefronts. Automattic maintains abuse reporting and a terms of service enforcement team, but the scale of the platform means some abuse goes undetected for periods. The free subdomain hosting model (name.wordpress.com) is a common vector.

Workable.description: Workable is the all-in-one hiring solution.
Workable.history: Workable was founded in 2012 in Athens, Greece, by Nikos Moraitakis and Spyros Magiatis. It has grown to become a widely-used applicant tracking and HR platform. The company raised significant VC funding and maintains offices in Greece, the UK, and the US.
Workable.summary_abuse: Workable has no notable abuse signal. It is an HR and recruiting SaaS platform used by businesses for hiring with no consumer-facing features that attract fraud.

Workadu.description: Workadu is a business management system designed to simplify operations and streamline processes for businesses.
Workadu.history: Workadu is a Greek business management and CRM platform designed to simplify operations for small businesses. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Workadu.summary_abuse: Workadu is a niche business management platform with no notable abuse signal.

Workarea.description: Workarea is a SaaS ecommerce platform for medium to large businesses.
Workarea.history: Workarea was an open-source e-commerce platform created by WebLinc in Philadelphia. VTEX acquired it in February 2021, integrating it into VTEX's broader enterprise e-commerce platform. Prior to that, BVAccel acquired WebLinc's internal commerce agency in 2019.
Workarea.summary_abuse: Workarea is an enterprise e-commerce platform now part of VTEX with no notable abuse signal. It targets mid-to-large retailers and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable by fraudsters.

Workbooks.description: Workbooks is a platform that helps manage sales leads, automate tasks, and track marketing efforts from advertisements to invoices.
Workbooks.history: Workbooks was founded in 2008 in Reading, UK, as a CRM and business management platform targeting growing UK SMEs. It remains independent and positions itself as a more affordable alternative to Salesforce.
Workbooks.summary_abuse: Workbooks is a UK-based CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a subscription B2B SaaS with no consumer-facing surface.

Workday.description: Workday is an enterprise cloud platform that provides solutions for finance, human resources, and business planning.
Workday.history: Workday was founded in 2005 by Dave Duffield and Aneel Bhusri, both former PeopleSoft executives, following Oracle's hostile acquisition of PeopleSoft in 2004. Workday went public on the NYSE in 2012 and grew to become the dominant enterprise HR and finance cloud platform.
Workday.summary_abuse: Workday has no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise ERP and HCM platform serving Fortune 500 companies, with comprehensive security controls and no consumer-facing surface attractive to fraudsters.

WORKetc.description: WORKetc is an all-in-one cloud computing platform offering web-based CRM, project management, and collaboration tools.
WORKetc.history: WORKetc was founded in 2009 in Australia as an all-in-one cloud platform combining CRM, project management, billing, and collaboration for small businesses. It has remained an independent SaaS company with no major ownership changes.
WORKetc.summary_abuse: WORKetc is a B2B CRM and project management platform with no notable abuse signal. It targets small businesses and does not expose consumer-facing surfaces commonly exploited for fraud.

Workstand.description: Workstand is a marketing and ecommerce solution designed for bike shops.
Workstand.history: Workstand is a marketing and e-commerce solution built specifically for independent bike shops, offering a vertical SaaS for the cycling retail industry.
Workstand.summary_abuse: Workstand is a niche vertical SaaS for bike shops with no notable abuse signal.

World4You.description: World4You operates homepage and domain solutions. World4Youu operates data centers in Austria and provides data protection.
World4You.history: World4You is an Austrian web hosting and domain registrar operating data centers in Austria. It focuses on data protection and GDPR compliance for Austrian and Central European customers.
World4You.summary_abuse: World4You is an Austrian web host and domain registrar. Hosting providers can be used to host phishing or malware sites, but World4You is a legitimate provider with Austrian data center operations and no unusual abuse reputation.

WorldPay.description: WorldPay is a merchant services and payment processing provider offering a payment gateway for online transactions.
WorldPay.history: Worldpay was originally founded in the UK in 1989, grew to become a major payment processor, and was acquired by FIS in 2019 for $43 billion. In 2023, FIS spun off Worldpay as a separate company and sold a majority stake to GTCR, though the brand continues under that ownership structure.
WorldPay.summary_abuse: Worldpay is a major legitimate payment processor. Like all payment gateways at scale, it may occasionally be used by fraudulent merchants, but it maintains PCI-DSS certification and active fraud detection. Its scale and enterprise focus mean abuse as a share of total volume is low.

WorldShopping.description: WorldShopping makes online purchases in Japan easier for international visitors.
WorldShopping.history: WorldShopping is a Japanese cross-border e-commerce enablement service that simplifies purchasing for international visitors shopping at Japanese online stores.
WorldShopping.summary_abuse: WorldShopping is a cross-border e-commerce service with no notable abuse signal. It facilitates international purchases at legitimate Japanese stores.

Worldz.description: Worldz calculates the economic value of a user’s social popularity (qualitatively and quantitatively). In proportion to this value, it provides a personalised discount, which can be applied in exchange for a social sharing by the user on their Instagram or Facebook profile.
Worldz.history: Worldz is an Italian social commerce tool that assigns economic value to a user's social media influence and offers personalised discounts in exchange for sharing on Instagram or Facebook.
Worldz.summary_abuse: Worldz is a social sharing discount tool with no notable abuse signal. It incentivises social media sharing and has no documented use in phishing or fraud.

WotNot.description: WotNot is a no-code chatbot platform enabling users to create and deploy chatbots without programming skills.
WotNot.history: WotNot is an Indian no-code chatbot platform founded around 2019, enabling businesses to deploy chatbots for customer support and lead generation without coding. It has grown to serve customers across multiple industries.
WotNot.summary_abuse: WotNot is a no-code chatbot platform with no notable abuse signal. It is primarily B2B-focused with no anonymous free consumer-facing surface.

WOW.description: Reveal CSS animation as you scroll down a page.
WOW.history: WOW.js is a JavaScript library created by Matthieu Aussaguel around 2013 that triggers CSS animations as users scroll down the page. It was widely used in the mid-2010s for creating scroll-reveal effects.
WOW.summary_abuse: WOW.js is a scroll-animation JavaScript library with no abuse signal. It is pure frontend library code with no data-handling surface.

Wowee.description: Wowee is a wishlist creation tool designed to help users organise and manage their desired items.
Wowee.history: Wowee is a Czech wishlist creation widget enabling users to organise desired items. It is a small consumer-facing utility tool with limited public history.
Wowee.summary_abuse: Wowee is a wishlist widget with no notable abuse signal.

Wowza Video Player.description: Wowza Video Player is a robust, industry standard player that provides HTML5, HLS, MPEG-DASH, and LL-DASH playback.
Wowza Video Player.history: Wowza Media Systems was founded in 2005 in Evergreen, Colorado, specializing in streaming media server software and services. Wowza Video Player is their HTML5 video player component offering HLS and adaptive bitrate streaming.
Wowza Video Player.summary_abuse: Wowza Video Player has no notable abuse signal. It is a professional video streaming tool used for legitimate media delivery with no consumer-facing attack surface for fraudsters.

WP Automatic.description: WP Automatic is a WordPress plugin that automates the process of creating posts on your WordPress site by automatically fetching content from various sources like RSS feeds, Amazon, eBay, ClickBank, and more.
WP Automatic.history: WP Automatic is a WordPress plugin that auto-publishes content scraped from RSS feeds, Amazon, eBay, and other sources. It has been available since around 2013 as a commercial plugin for automated content publishing.
WP Automatic.summary_abuse: WP Automatic has a documented abuse profile as a content scraping and auto-publishing plugin. It is commonly used on spam or affiliate content farms that aggregate scraped content to generate ad revenue or SEO signals. Sites running WP Automatic at scale are often low-quality spam sites, and the plugin is a known vector in content scraping campaigns. The plugin itself has no hosted consumer surface, but its primary use case in practice leans toward content spam.

WP Courseware.description: WP Courseware is an LMS plugin that allows novice and professional users to build online courses using its easy-to-use drag and drop course builder.
WP Courseware.history: WP Courseware is a WordPress LMS plugin launched around 2012 that allows creating and selling online courses. It is developed by Fly Plugins and has remained a niche but stable product in the WordPress LMS market.
WP Courseware.summary_abuse: WP Courseware is a WordPress LMS plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is course-building software for legitimate online educators, not a platform commonly exploited for fraud.

WP Engine.description: WP Engine is a website hosting provider.
WP Engine.history: WP Engine was founded in 2010 by Jason Cohen in Austin, Texas as a managed WordPress hosting platform. It grew into the leading managed WordPress host, raising significant VC funding. In 2024, WP Engine became embroiled in a public dispute with Automattic and WordPress.org founder Matt Mullenweg over trademark usage and contributions to open source.
WP Engine.summary_abuse: WP Engine is a managed WordPress hosting provider with moderate abuse exposure typical of web hosts. Malicious sites and phishing pages are occasionally hosted on the platform, and WP Engine publishes an AUP with abuse reporting channels. Its position as a premium managed host generally means lower abuse rates than commodity shared hosting.

WP Fastest Cache.description: WP Fastest Cache is one of a number of plugins for WordPress designed to accelerate the performance of your website.
WP Fastest Cache.history: WP Fastest Cache was developed by Emre Vona in Turkey around 2013 as a WordPress caching plugin with a focus on simplicity. It became one of the most popular free caching options on WordPress.org.
WP Fastest Cache.summary_abuse: WP Fastest Cache is a WordPress caching plugin with no abuse signal. It is backend performance infrastructure that accelerates WordPress sites with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

WP Featherlight.description: WP Featherlight is a WordPress lightbox plugin for adding a minimal, high-performance, responsive jQuery lightbox to your WordPress website.
WP Featherlight.history: WP Featherlight is a free WordPress lightbox plugin that wraps the Featherlight.js jQuery library for use in WordPress. Created around 2015 as a simple utility plugin.
WP Featherlight.summary_abuse: WP Featherlight is a WordPress lightbox plugin with no abuse signal. It is a simple frontend plugin with no data handling or hosted service surface.

WP Google Map Embed.description: WP Google Map Embed is a lightweight WordPress plugin for embedding Google Maps with custom markers via shortcodes in posts, pages, and sidebars.
WP Google Map Embed.history: WP Google Map Embed is a lightweight free WordPress plugin for embedding Google Maps via shortcode, created around 2014. No notable ownership changes.
WP Google Map Embed.summary_abuse: WP Google Map Embed is a simple Google Maps embedding plugin with no abuse signal. It is a small utility WordPress plugin with no data collection beyond what Google Maps itself provides.

WP Google Map Plugin.description: WP Google Map Plugin allows you to create google maps shortcodes to display responsive google maps on pages, widgets and custom templates.
WP Google Map Plugin.history: WP Google Map Plugin is a WordPress plugin for creating and displaying Google Maps via shortcodes, with a free and premium version. Created around 2012 and maintained as a niche WordPress utility.
WP Google Map Plugin.summary_abuse: WP Google Map Plugin is a Google Maps embed plugin for WordPress with no abuse signal. It is a utility plugin that embeds Google Maps on pages with no data-handling surface beyond Google Maps API calls.

WP Grid Builder.description: WP Grid Builder is a WordPress plugin that enables the creation of advanced grid layouts.
WP Grid Builder.history: WP Grid Builder is a premium WordPress plugin for creating advanced filterable grid layouts. It was released around 2019 and is primarily maintained as a solo developer commercial plugin.
WP Grid Builder.summary_abuse: WP Grid Builder is a WordPress layout plugin with no abuse signal. It is developer tooling for creating WordPress page layouts with no data-handling surface.

WP Job Openings.description: WP Job Openings is a job listing and recruitment plugin for WordPress websites.
WP Job Openings.history: WP Job Openings is a WordPress plugin for adding job listings to websites, developed around 2018 by Awsm Innovations in India. It has a free version on WordPress.org and a premium version with additional features.
WP Job Openings.summary_abuse: WP Job Openings is a WordPress job listing plugin with minimal abuse signal. It could theoretically be used to post fake job listings, but this is a content moderation concern rather than systematic platform abuse.

WP Live Visitor Counter.description: WP Live Visitor Counter is a WordPress plugin that displays the number of online visitors on a website in real-time.
WP Live Visitor Counter.history: WP Live Visitor Counter is a simple WordPress plugin that shows real-time visitor counts. It is a small utility plugin with no notable history.
WP Live Visitor Counter.summary_abuse: WP Live Visitor Counter is a simple WordPress visitor counter widget with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend display plugin with minimal data handling.

WP Maintenance Mode.description: WP Maintenance Mode is a WordPress plugin which add a maintenance page to your blog.
WP Maintenance Mode.history: WP Maintenance Mode is a WordPress plugin for displaying maintenance and coming-soon pages, created around 2011. It has been widely adopted as one of the most downloaded free maintenance page plugins on WordPress.org.
WP Maintenance Mode.summary_abuse: WP Maintenance Mode is a WordPress maintenance page plugin with no abuse signal. It is backend administrative tooling with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

WP Portfolio.description: WP Portfolio is a premium standalone WordPress plugin designed for creating and showcasing portfolios.
WP Portfolio.history: WP Portfolio is a premium WordPress plugin for creating and showcasing portfolios. It is a niche plugin targeted at creative professionals using WordPress.
WP Portfolio.summary_abuse: WP Portfolio is a WordPress portfolio plugin with no abuse signal. It is self-hosted portfolio display software with no data-handling surface exploitable for fraud.

WP Puzzle Basic.description: WP Puzzle Basic is fully responsive, clean and minimal WordPress theme.
WP Puzzle Basic.history: WP Puzzle Basic is a minimalist responsive free WordPress theme. No notable history or ownership changes on record.
WP Puzzle Basic.summary_abuse: WP Puzzle Basic is a free WordPress theme with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted site template with no hosted service surface.

WP Rocket.description: WP Rocket is a caching and performance optimisation plugin to improve the loading speed of WordPress websites.
WP Rocket.history: WP Rocket was founded in 2013 in France by WP Media as a premium WordPress caching and performance plugin. It became the most popular paid performance plugin in the WordPress ecosystem, known for its ease of use and comprehensive optimization features.
WP Rocket.summary_abuse: WP Rocket is a premium WordPress caching plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is backend performance infrastructure that does not provide hosting or communication services that fraudsters would exploit.

WP SEO AI.description: WP SEO AI is a tool that optimizes brand visibility by positioning companies as expert answers across search engines and AI platforms like Google and GPT.
WP SEO AI.history: WP SEO AI is a recently launched SaaS tool (circa 2023) that positions itself as an AI-powered SEO optimization platform for WordPress sites. It has limited public history.
WP SEO AI.summary_abuse: WP SEO AI is an AI-powered SEO tool for WordPress with no documented abuse signal. SEO tools can be misused for search-spam campaigns, though this is a general pattern for the SEO tool category rather than a specific documented issue with this product.

WP-Optimize.description: WP-Optimize is an all-in-one WordPress plugin that cleans your database, compresses your large images and caches your site.
WP-Optimize.history: WP-Optimize was developed by Team Updraft (the same team behind UpdraftPlus) in the UK and has been available since around 2012. It combines database cleaning, image compression, and caching in a single WordPress plugin.
WP-Optimize.summary_abuse: WP-Optimize is a WordPress performance plugin with no abuse signal. It is backend infrastructure for optimizing WordPress databases and images with no consumer-facing surface.

WP-PageNavi.description: WP-PageNavi is a WordPress plugin which adds a more advanced paging navigation interface to your WordPress blog.
WP-PageNavi.history: WP-PageNavi is a free WordPress pagination plugin created around 2007 that adds numbered page navigation to WordPress themes. It became one of the most widely used WordPress pagination plugins.
WP-PageNavi.summary_abuse: WP-PageNavi is a WordPress pagination plugin with no abuse signal. It is a simple frontend utility plugin with no data handling.

WP-Royal Ashe.description: WP-Royal Ashe is a personal and multi-author WordPress blog theme.
WP-Royal Ashe.history: WP-Royal Ashe is a free personal and multi-author blog WordPress theme by WP Royal, a small theme development shop. No notable ownership changes or history.
WP-Royal Ashe.summary_abuse: WP-Royal Ashe is a free WordPress blog theme with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted site template with no hosted service surface.

WP-Royal Bard.description: WP-Royal Bard is a personal and multi-author WordPress blog theme.
WP-Royal Bard.history: WP-Royal Bard is a free personal and multi-author blog WordPress theme by WP Royal, the same small theme shop behind the Ashe theme. No notable ownership changes or history.
WP-Royal Bard.summary_abuse: WP-Royal Bard is a free WordPress blog theme with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted site template with no hosted service surface.

WP-Statistics.description: WP-Statistics is a WordPress plugin which allows you to know your website statistics.
WP-Statistics.history: WP-Statistics is a self-hosted WordPress analytics plugin created around 2010, developed by VeronaLabs (previously by Mostafa Moradi). It positions itself as a GDPR-friendly Google Analytics alternative that keeps data on your own server.
WP-Statistics.summary_abuse: WP-Statistics is a self-hosted WordPress analytics plugin with no abuse signal. Analytics data stays on the user's own server, making it privacy-positive compared to cloud analytics, with no cross-site tracking or data broker exposure.

wp.cloud.history: wp.cloud is a WordPress-optimized cloud hosting platform, likely associated with Automattic (the company behind WordPress.com). It offers WordPress-first PaaS-style hosting infrastructure. The platform appears to be a relatively new offering.
wp.cloud.summary_abuse: wp.cloud is a WordPress hosting platform with a low abuse signal. As a managed hosting provider, it could theoretically host malicious WordPress sites, a risk inherent to all managed hosting. No specific documented abuse patterns distinguish it from typical managed hosting providers.

wpBakery.description: WPBakery is a drag and drop visual page builder plugin for WordPress.
wpBakery.history: WPBakery (originally Visual Composer) was created by WPBakery in Slovakia around 2011 and became one of the best-selling WordPress page builder plugins, with millions of installs. It was sold as a one-time-purchase plugin through Envato's ThemeForest marketplace and later bundled with many premium WordPress themes. In 2018 the product was rebranded from Visual Composer to WPBakery Page Builder.
wpBakery.summary_abuse: WPBakery is a widely-used WordPress page builder plugin with minimal abuse signal. It is installed on millions of legitimate WordPress sites. It does not provide hosting or file upload features directly, though it enables page creation that could be used for malicious content if the underlying WordPress installation is compromised. No specific abuse patterns are documented for the plugin itself.

wpCache.description: WPCache is a static caching plugin for WordPress.
wpCache.history: WPCache (wp-cache) is one of the earliest WordPress page caching plugins, created circa 2005 as a static file caching mechanism. Its last release was in September 2007 and the plugin is largely superseded by more modern alternatives such as WP Super Cache and W3 Total Cache which built on its concepts.
wpCache.summary_abuse: wpCache is a legacy WordPress caching plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend performance tool that caches static HTML pages and has no consumer-facing surface that scammers would target.

WPCacheOn.description: WPCacheOn is a caching and performance optimisation plugin, which improves the loading speed of WordPress websites.
WPCacheOn.history: WPCacheOn is a caching and performance plugin for WordPress, created around 2018 as a lightweight alternative to more established caching plugins. It is a small independent plugin with limited public history.
WPCacheOn.summary_abuse: WPCacheOn is a WordPress caching plugin with no abuse signal. It is backend performance infrastructure with no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

WPForms.description: WPForms is a drag and drop WordPress form builder.
WPForms.history: WPForms was created in 2016 by Syed Balkhi and Jared Atchison under the Awesome Motive company umbrella, designed as a beginner-friendly drag-and-drop form builder for WordPress. It rapidly became one of the most popular WordPress form plugins, surpassing millions of active installs.
WPForms.summary_abuse: WPForms has low abuse risk as a form-builder plugin; it processes form submissions server-side on the site owner's WordPress installation and does not offer consumer-facing hosting with anonymous signup. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates and the plugin is a standard tool for contact forms, lead generation, and surveys on WordPress sites. No notable incidents of the plugin itself being weaponized for large-scale fraud.

WPML.description: WPML plugin makes it possible to build and run fully multilingual WordPress sites.
WPML.history: WPML (WordPress Multilingual Plugin) was created around 2008 by OnTheGoSystems, an Israeli company, and became the dominant commercial multilingual solution for WordPress. It has remained independently maintained under OnTheGoSystems, with no major ownership changes, building a large user base across enterprise and agency WordPress deployments.
WPML.summary_abuse: WPML has minimal abuse signal; it is a backend WordPress plugin for multilingual content management and does not expose a consumer-facing hosted surface. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates across global e-commerce and publishing sites. No notable fraud or scam patterns associated with the plugin.

WPMU DEV Smush.description: WPMU DEV Smush is a WordPress plugin that allows you to optimise images without losing quality.
WPMU DEV Smush.history: Smush (originally WP Smush.it) is an image compression WordPress plugin developed by WPMU DEV, a company that has offered a suite of WordPress tools since the late 2000s. It became one of the most-installed image optimization plugins in the WordPress ecosystem, with both a free version on WordPress.org and a premium Pro version.
WPMU DEV Smush.summary_abuse: WPMU DEV Smush has no notable abuse signal; it is a server-side image optimization plugin for WordPress with no consumer-facing hosted surface that fraudsters would target.

WPS Visitor Counter.description: WPS Visitor Counter is a plugin for WordPress that counts the number of visitors to a website.
WPS Visitor Counter.history: WPS Visitor Counter is a WordPress plugin that counts website visitors; it is a simple utility plugin available on WordPress.org with free and premium tiers.
WPS Visitor Counter.summary_abuse: WPS Visitor Counter is a WordPress visitor counting plugin with no abuse signal. It is a self-hosted analytics utility with no consumer-facing surface.

WSHOP.description: WSHOP is a cloud ecommerce platform for managing online stores, transactions, and customer interactions.
WSHOP.history: WSHOP was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in Paris, France. The company provides a SaaS Unified Digital Commerce platform for managing online stores, physical retail, multilingual catalogs, POS, and CRM tools. WSHOP is backed by CAPZA, a European investment firm. The platform claims over 17 years of combined team experience in digital commerce.
WSHOP.summary_abuse: WSHOP is a B2B ecommerce SaaS platform aimed at retailers and enterprises with no notable abuse signal. The platform requires merchant setup and is not freely accessible to anonymous users. No documented phishing, fraud, or scam incidents are associated with WSHOP.

Wt.description: Wt is a C++ library for developing web applications.
Wt.history: Wt (pronounced witty) is a C++ library for developing web applications, created by Emweb. It provides a widget-based approach to building web UIs in C++, with backends for various databases and frameworks.
Wt.summary_abuse: Wt has no abuse signal. It is a C++ backend web framework library with no consumer-facing surface.

WuBook.description: WuBook is a software platform that allows efficient management of rooms and rates for hospitality operations.
WuBook.history: WuBook is an Italian hospitality software platform for managing rooms, rates, and reservations, serving hotels, B&Bs, and vacation rentals primarily in Europe.
WuBook.summary_abuse: WuBook is a B2B hospitality management platform with no notable abuse signal.

Wufoo.description: Wufoo is an online form builder that creates forms including contact forms, online payments, online surveys and event registrations.
Wufoo.history: Wufoo was founded in 2006 by Chris Campbell, Kevin Hale, and Ryan Campbell as an online form builder. It was acquired by SurveyMonkey (now Momentive) in April 2011. The product has been maintained under SurveyMonkey ownership but has received less active development as the parent company focuses on its core survey product.
Wufoo.summary_abuse: Wufoo forms have historically been exploited for phishing credential-harvesting pages and fake data-collection schemes due to the ease of creating public forms with no KYC requirements. The platform has a free tier allowing anonymous form creation. Momentive maintains an abuse reporting mechanism, but Wufoo-hosted phishing forms are a documented pattern in security research.

Wuilt.description: Wuilt is the first Arab platform of its kind to help individuals and businesses create ready-made websites and ecommerce stores.
Wuilt.history: Wuilt is an Arab-focused website builder and e-commerce platform enabling individuals and businesses in the Middle East and North Africa to create ready-made websites.
Wuilt.summary_abuse: Wuilt is an Arab website builder with a free tier. As a hosted page builder it carries some inherent risk for hosting scam pages, though no specific documented incidents are known.

Wunderkind.description: Wunderkind (Formerly BounceX) is a software for behavioural marketing technologies, created to de-anonymise site visitors, analyse their digital behaviour and create relevant digital experiences regardless of channel or device.
Wunderkind.history: Wunderkind was founded in 2010 as BounceX, a behavioral marketing startup. It rebranded to Wunderkind in 2020. The company raised $156M in total funding including a $76M Series C in March 2023. It specializes in identifying anonymous website visitors for email and SMS retargeting.
Wunderkind.summary_abuse: Wunderkind is an identity-resolution and behavioral retargeting platform that tracks visitors across sites to build cross-site identity graphs. Its core business involves extensive data collection and targeting of individuals without explicit per-site consent, which has attracted privacy criticism. No documented use for direct fraud or phishing, but its heavy tracker density and identity-resolution capabilities raise privacy concerns.

Wurfl.description: WURFL.js is JavaScript that detects device models of smartphones, tablets, smart TVs and game consoles accessing your website.
Wurfl.history: WURFL (Wireless Universal Resource FiLe) originated as an open community XML database for mobile device capabilities. ScientiaMobile was founded in June 2011 by Luca Passani (WURFL's creator) and Steve Kamerman to commercialise it. WURFL.js is the JavaScript client for in-browser device detection used by major players including Google, Amazon, and Akamai.
Wurfl.summary_abuse: WURFL is a device detection library with no notable abuse signal. It is an infrastructure tool used by major web platforms to identify device types and has no consumer-facing surface.

WWPass.description: WWPass is a solution that enables authentication without the use of usernames, simplifying the login process while maintaining security through alternative methods.
WWPass.history: WWPass was founded around 2007 and offers a passwordless authentication solution using hardware tokens and mobile keys. It has operated as a niche security product targeting enterprise authentication use cases.
WWPass.summary_abuse: WWPass is an authentication service with no notable abuse signal. Its purpose is to reduce credential-based attacks rather than enable them, and it has no consumer-facing hosting surface associated with fraud.

Wx3.description: Wx3 is a Brazilian e-commerce platform for fashion and retail stores.
Wx3.history: Wx3 is a Brazilian e-commerce platform serving fashion and retail stores. It is a regional vertical SaaS with limited public history outside Brazil.
Wx3.summary_abuse: Wx3 is a niche Brazilian fashion e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal.

Wyng.description: Wyng is a platform that connects audiences and drives consumer actions across various marketing channels.
Wyng.history: Wyng is a US enterprise marketing activation platform enabling personalized digital experiences and consumer data collection across channels. It targets large brand marketers and has evolved from its origins in social marketing.
Wyng.summary_abuse: Wyng is an enterprise marketing platform with no notable abuse signal. It is an enterprise-only B2B tool with no consumer-facing free tier.

Wyre.description: Wyre is a financial technology platform that enables cryptocurrency companies to connect digital assets with traditional fiat payment systems.
Wyre.history: Wyre was founded in 2013 as a fintech platform bridging cryptocurrency and traditional fiat payments. It reached a $1.5 billion valuation in 2022 when Bolt attempted to acquire it, but the deal fell through amid the crypto market downturn. Wyre shut down in early 2023 after the failed acquisition and instructed users to withdraw assets by July 2023.
Wyre.summary_abuse: Wyre was a crypto-fiat payment processor that shut down in 2023. Crypto payment processors carry moderate abuse risk due to potential use in illicit transactions, but Wyre was a VC-backed US company targeting legitimate crypto businesses. No specific documented fraud incidents are known for the platform itself.

WysiBB.description: WysiBB very simple and functional open-source WYSIWYG BBCode editor based on jQuery.
WysiBB.history: WysiBB is a simple open-source WYSIWYG BBCode editor for web applications built on jQuery. It has been largely dormant as BBCode has fallen out of favor in modern web applications.
WysiBB.summary_abuse: WysiBB has no abuse signal. It is a small dormant JavaScript editor library with no consumer-facing surface.
